summary: Implemented a repository-local token/context governance standard for Codex and Claude Code. The change adds a deterministic specbridge-token-governance-status command, a source-backed policy artifact, operational documentation, file-backed status evidence, CLI regression coverage, and a defensive doctor fix that prevents empty gh PR results from producing a malformed branch_convention_violation:# warning.
validations: Claude Code auxiliary review: no blocking findings
GitHub Actions SpecBridge Review Gate: passed
GitHub Actions SpecBridge PR Review Report: passed
GitHub Actions Claude Review Non Blocking: passed
policy_result: Within policy. Work stayed inside issue-224 contract and scope. The command reads local repository policy only and does not launch Claude Code, launch Codex, call the network, mutate GitHub, inspect secrets, change billing, change CI/CD security, enforce cleanup, or deploy. No protected files, provider tokens, API keys, raw hidden prompts, raw ChatGPT transcripts, production configuration, authentication, authorization, database, dependency, workflow, cleanup enforcement, artifact retention enforcement, issue #194, or digital twin changes are included.
risk_result: Medium risk and contained. The change standardizes token/context governance and touches CLI routing, tests, and doctor diagnostics. The status command is deterministic and read-only unless OutputPath is explicitly provided. The doctor fix only filters null or empty PR objects before branch convention checks. The policy records --max-turns as a conditional current-provider standard because the installed local Claude Code CLI may not expose it yet; live runtime launches must not add it until capability detection confirms support.
unresolved_risks: Live runner enforcement of --max-turns remains a future governed task because the installed local Claude Code CLI help does not expose that flag in this environment.
Provider documentation may change and must be refreshed through a future governed contract.
merge_status: PR #225 merged on 2026-06-16 at merge commit 442c98307c53d21c814c40a9235af16e18e9c858. Issue #224 closed automatically and post-merge closure evidence was recorded.
